name: Honey Almond Whole Wheat Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop almonds. Note: other nuts can be substituted.
2. Crumble yeast if in cake form and add into warm water; stir until dissolved.
3. Stir in brown sugar and honey. Stir in eggs, salt, melted butter, orange rind, and buttermilk (or sour milk).
4. Mix all flours with all but 2 tablespoons of nuts; stir into liquid mixture.
5. Let this very soft dough rest for 10 minutes.
6. Scrape dough onto a board or pastry cloth dusted with about 1/2 cup additional all-purpose flour.
7. Knead for about 5 minutes, working in flour on the board, until dough is elastic but still soft and tender.
8. Place dough in a greased bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm place about 2 1/2 hours, or until doubled in bulk.
9. Turn dough onto a floured board again; gently pat into a 12x10 inch rectangle.
10. Sprinkle brown sugar filling over dough and roll up from the 10-inch side.
11. Fold two ends under to make a ball-shaped loaf and place on a greased baking sheet.
12. Brush with melted butter. Fold a 36-inch piece of foil to make a 2-inch wide strip (36 inches long).
13. Fasten with paper clips into a ring and set ring around the dough.
14. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm oven until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
15. Sprinkle loaf with remaining chopped nuts (and some butter and brown sugar if you like) and bake at 350°F for 1 hour or until golden brown. Makes one 3 3/4 pound loaf. Cut into halves or quarters and freeze separately, if desired.
Brown Sugar Filling:
Mix 1/2 cup packed brown sugar with 1 tablespoon cinnamon and 2 tablespoons wheat germ. I also add some finely chopped nuts.
